behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff (reject + do-not-forward), not only HMAC math. A unit test of signature verification alone could miss accidental enqueue on reject. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP are not required. Higher levels rejected because behavior text only needs in-process HTTP handler + fake queue interaction, not real network/OS/model output.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the HTTP handler with a fake queue module. Send a request with mismatched body/signature and assert `401` plus zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across modules (handler response lifecycle vs queue write completion). In-process wiring can reproduce missing `await`/ordering bugs. System rejected: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess; Agentic/Workflow not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler + test queue adapter that records write completion timestamp/hook. Assert queue write completes before response is finalized as `200`.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is decoupling between enqueue/ack and downstream processor completion (cross-module async handoff). A single-function unit test can miss integration waiting points. System rejected because real OS/network is not required to expose the wait-vs-no-wait bug.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a downstream processor stub that blocks on a controllable promise. Send request, assert response returns before releasing processor promise.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency, which is sensitive to real HTTP server/event-loop scheduling and runtime behavior. In-process fakes can hide queueing/backpressure timing effects. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI-model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real Node HTTP server process. Create concurrent load where earlier events trigger slow downstream work, then measure end-to-end client-observed latency for new requests and assert responses stay under 3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup is a state-handoff/persistence behavior across handler + queue storage, not just pure local logic. Needs module wiring with shared state (e.g., repo/DB-backed queue). System rejected because local in-process integration with local persistence is sufficient; no real external network/OS primitive required.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler against queue/repository test instance with persisted seen IDs. Send same event ID twice and assert only one queue record/write occurs.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure is contract mapping between queue-full signal and HTTP response fields (cross-module boundary). A fake queue that returns `full` can reproduce it. System rejected because real OS/network conditions are not required by behavior text; Agentic/Workflow not relevant.
behaviors.test_description: Inject queue module configured to return capacity-exceeded/full. Send valid request and assert `503` plus `Retry-After` header and no successful enqueue.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue implementation behavior with persistence/state, typically requiring queue module + storage layer interaction. Unit may miss persistence/count synchronization issues. System considered but rejected: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess/OS-specific primitive beyond local persistence wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real queue component against a local test persistence backend, set capacity N, enqueue N events successfully, then assert N+1 is rejected with the expected full/capacity error.
